1329	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
1330	
1331	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
1332	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
1333	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
1334	
1335	section Dual
1336	
1337	open InternalHom Opposite
1338	
1339	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1340	
1341	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
1342	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
1343	over itself / the regular representation). The value
1344	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
1345	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
1346	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
1347	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
1348	the varying structure sheaf. -/
1349	noncomputable def dual
1350	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1351	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1352	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1353	
1354	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
1355	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
1356	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
1357	noncomputable def evalLin
1358	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1359	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1360	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
1361	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1362	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1363	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
1364	
1365	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
1366	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
1367	lemma evalLin_add
1368	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1369	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
1370	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
1371	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
1372	
1373	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
1374	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
1375	lemma evalLin_smul
1376	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1377	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1378	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
1379	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
1380	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
1381	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
1382	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
1383	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
1384	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1385	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1386	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
1387	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
1388	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
1389	  rfl
1390	
1391	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
1392	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
1393	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
1394	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its
1395	terminal section, applied to `s`. Bilinearity over `R(X)`: linearity in `s` is linearity
1396	of `evalLin`; linearity in `φ` is `evalLin_add` / `evalLin_smul`. The codomain is cast to
1397	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
1398	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
1399	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
1400	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
1401	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
1402	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1403	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
1404	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
1405	  ModuleCat.ofHom
1406	    (TensorProduct.lift
1407	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1408	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
1409	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
1410	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
1411	        (fun s φ φ' => by
1412	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
1413	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
1414	        (fun c s φ => by
1415	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
1416	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))
1417	
1418	end Dual
1419	
1420	end PresheafOfModules

1428	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1429	
1430	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1431	
1432	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1433	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1434	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1435	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1436	
1437	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1438	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1439	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1440	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1441	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1442	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1443	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1444	
1445	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1446	
1447	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1448	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1449	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1450	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1451	
1452	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1453	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1454	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1455	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1456	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1457	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1458	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1459	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1460	
1461	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1462	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1463	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1464	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1465	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1466	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1467	law is definitional. -/
1468	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1469	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1470	
1471	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1472	
1473	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1474	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1475	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1476	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1477	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1478	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1479	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1480	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1481	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1482	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1483	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1484	
1485	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1486	
1487	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1488	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1489	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1490	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1491	
1492	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1493	
1494	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1495	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1496	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1497	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1498	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1499	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1500	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1501	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1502	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1503	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1504	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1505	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1506	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1507	
1508	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1509	sheaf.**
1510	
1511	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1512	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1513	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1514	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1515	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1516	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1517	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1518	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1519	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1520	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1521	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
1522	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1523	
1524	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1525	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
1526	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
1527	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
1528	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
1529	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1530	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
1531	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1532	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1533	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1534	
1535	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1536	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
1537	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the

1539	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :

1542	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1543	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1544	
1545	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
1546	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)

1004	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

1006	
1007	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1008	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1009	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1010	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1011	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1012	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1013	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1014	
1015	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1016	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1017	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1018	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1019	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1020	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1021	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1022	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1023	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1024	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1025	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1026	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1027	
1028	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1029	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1030	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1031	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1032	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1033	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1034	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1035	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1036	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1037	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
1038	    (by
1039	      intro X Y g; ext m
1040	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
1041	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
1042	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
1043	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
1044	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
1045	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)
1046	
1047	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1048	
1049	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
1050	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
1051	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1052	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1053	
1054	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1055	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1056	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1057	
1058	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1059	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1060	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1061	
1062	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1063	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1064	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1065	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1066	
1067	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
1068	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1069	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1070	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1071	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1072	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1073	
1074	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1075	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1076	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1077	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1078	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1079	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1080	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1081	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1082	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1083	@[implicit_reducible]
1084	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1085	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1086	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1087	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1088	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1089	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1090	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1091	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1092	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1093	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1094	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1095	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1096	  add_smul f g φ := by
1097	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1098	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
1099	
1100	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1101	
1102	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1103	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
1104	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1105	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
1106	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
1107	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
1108	applied to that terminal. -/
1109	
1110	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1111	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1112	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1113	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1114	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1115	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1116	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1117	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1118	
1119	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1120	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1121	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1122	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1123	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1124	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
1125	@[implicit_reducible]
1126	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
1127	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1128	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
1129	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
1130	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
1131	
1132	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
1133	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
1134	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
1135	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
1136	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
1137	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
1138	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/

1140	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1141	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
1142	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
1143	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ

1145	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
1146	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/

1148	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1149	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1150	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
1151	  ext1 X; rfl
1152	
1153	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
1154	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)

1157	  ext1 X; rfl

1159	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*

1161	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in

1163	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}

1165	  subst h; rfl

1167	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
1168	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
1169	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}

1171	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
1172	  ext1 X
1173	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
1174	
1175	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,

1177	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
1178	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1179	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
1180	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
1181	  ext1 X
1182	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
1183	
1184	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
1185	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
1186	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1187	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1188	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
1189	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
1190	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
1191	
1192	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
1193	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
1194	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
1195	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
1196	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
1197	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
1198	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1199	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
1200	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
1201	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
1202	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
1203	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
1204	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
1205	  ext1 Y
1206	  ext m
1207	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1208	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
1209	  congr 1
1210	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
1211	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
1212	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
1213	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
1214	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
1215	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1216	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
1217	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
1218	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
1219	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
1220	    rw [e1, e2]
1221	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
1222	  rw [hmor]
1223	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
1224	  rfl

1228	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
1229	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
1230	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1231	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
1232	  toFun := restrictionMap g
1233	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
1234	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
1235	
1236	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
1237	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
1238	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1239	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
1240	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
1241	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1242	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
1243	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
1244	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
1245	  map_id X := by
1246	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1247	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1248	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
1249	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
1250	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
1251	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
1252	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
1253	
1254	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
1255	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
1256	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
1257	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
1258	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
1259	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
1260	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
1261	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
1262	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1263	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
1264	    restrictionMap f.unop
1265	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
1266	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
1267	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
1268	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
1269	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
1270	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
1271	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
1272	
1273	end InternalHom

1275	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1276	
1277	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1278	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1279	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1280	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1281	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1282	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1283	
1284	namespace InternalHom
1285	
1286	section Assembly
1287

988	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
989	
990	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
991	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
992	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
993	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
994	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
995	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
996	project-local supplement. -/

1011	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/

1017	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1018	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1019	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1020	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1021	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1022	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1023	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1024	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1025	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1026	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1027

the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.

The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
project-local supplement. -/

namespace InternalHom

open CategoryTheory Limits

universe vC uC

variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op

/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl

/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
    (by
      intro X Y g; ext m
      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)

variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}

/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
`termRingMap Y f`. -/
lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl

/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl

/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl

/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl

/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]

/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
@[implicit_reducible]
noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
  mul_smul f g φ := by
    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
  smul_add f φ ψ := by
    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
  add_smul f g φ := by
    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]

/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`

The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
applied to that terminal. -/

/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
noncomputable def restr (U : C)
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M

/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
@[implicit_reducible]
noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
    (restr U M) (restr U N)

/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ

/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
  ext1 X; rfl

/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
  ext1 X; rfl

/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
  subst h; rfl

/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
  ext1 X
  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))

/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
  ext1 X
  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))

/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
`pushforward₀.map`). -/
lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
  Functor.map_comp _ _ _

/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
  ext1 Y
  ext m
  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
  congr 1
  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
    rw [e1, e2]
    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
  rw [hmor]
  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
  rfl

/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
  toFun := restrictionMap g
  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g

/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
  map_id X := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
    exact restrictionMap_id φ
  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ

/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
    restrictionMap f.unop
        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
    = restrictionMap f.unop m
        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]

end InternalHom

/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base

`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/

namespace InternalHom

section Assembly

variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):

1898 AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean
the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.

The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
project-local supplement. -/

namespace InternalHom

open CategoryTheory Limits

universe vC uC

variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op

/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl

/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
    (by
      intro X Y g; ext m
      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)

variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}

/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
`termRingMap Y f`. -/
lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl

/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl

/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl

/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl

/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]

/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
@[implicit_reducible]
noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
  mul_smul f g φ := by
    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
  smul_add f φ ψ := by
    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
  add_smul f g φ := by
    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]

/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`

The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
applied to that terminal. -/

/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
noncomputable def restr (U : C)
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M

/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
@[implicit_reducible]
noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
    (restr U M) (restr U N)

/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ

/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
  ext1 X; rfl

/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
  ext1 X; rfl

/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
  subst h; rfl

/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
  ext1 X
  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))

/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
  ext1 X
  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))

/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
`pushforward₀.map`). -/
lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
  Functor.map_comp _ _ _

/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
  ext1 Y
  ext m
  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
  congr 1
  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
    rw [e1, e2]
    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
  rw [hmor]
  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
  rfl

/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
  toFun := restrictionMap g
  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g

/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
  map_id X := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
    exact restrictionMap_id φ
  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ

/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
    restrictionMap f.unop
        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
    = restrictionMap f.unop m
        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]

end InternalHom

/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base

`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/

namespace InternalHom

section Assembly

variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):

1429	
1430	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1431	
1432	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1433	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1434	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1435	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1436	
1437	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1438	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1439	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1440	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1441	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1442	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1443	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1444	
1445	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1446	
1447	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1448	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1449	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1450	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1451	
1452	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1453	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1454	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1455	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1456	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1457	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1458	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1459	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)

1461	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1462	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1463	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1464	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1465	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1466	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1467	law is definitional. -/
1468	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1469	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- Value of `internalHomEvalApp` on a simple tensor: the contraction `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`.
The eval value is kept at its NATURAL over-ring type `R₀.obj X` (not ascribed to the
unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`). Used to reduce the naturality of `internalHomEval`. -/
@[simp] lemma internalHomEvalApp_tmul
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
    (s : (M.obj X : Type u)) (φ : ((dual M).obj X : Type u)) :
    (internalHomEvalApp M X).hom (s ⊗ₜ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] φ) = evalLin M X φ s :=
  rfl

/-- **The evaluation morphism `ev_M : M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`** (blueprint `lem:internal_hom_eval`):
the full natural morphism of presheaves of modules with `app X := internalHomEvalApp M X`
(the per-open contraction `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`). Naturality is the per-open contraction commuting
with the further-restriction maps; it reduces (via `tensor_ext` on the simple tensor) to the
`PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply` of the dual section `φ` along the over-category morphism
`Over.homMk f.unop`. Project-local: the evaluation pairing of the project's `PresheafOfModules`
internal hom / dual, the contraction half of the `⊗`-inverse datum. -/
noncomputable def internalHomEval
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶
      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
    intro X Y f
    sorry)

end Dual

{"file_path":"/home/archon/.elan/toolchains/leanprover--lean4---v4.30.0-rc2/src/lean/Lean/Elab/Declaration.lean","content":"/-
